questions.question: What is Error Handling & Debugging and why is it important?
questions.answer: Error Handling & Debugging is a fundamental concept that try/catch, error types, console methods, and debugging techniques. It's important because it forms the foundation for building robust and efficient applications. Understanding Error Handling & Debugging allows developers to make better architectural decisions and write cleaner code.

questions.question: How do you apply Error Handling & Debugging in a real project?
questions.answer: In practice, Error Handling & Debugging is applied by following established patterns and best practices. Start with the simplest implementation, test thoroughly, and iterate. The key is to understand the underlying principles so you can adapt to different scenarios.

questions.question: What are common mistakes when working with Error Handling & Debugging?
questions.answer: Common mistakes include: not understanding the fundamentals before applying advanced patterns, ignoring edge cases, not testing adequately, and over-engineering solutions. The best approach is to start simple and add complexity only when needed.

questions.question: How would you explain Error Handling & Debugging to a beginner?
questions.answer: Error Handling & Debugging is essentially about try/catch, error types, console methods, and debugging techniques. Think of it as a building block — once you understand this concept, you can combine it with other concepts to build complex applications.
